title: Psychotria andetrensis
intro: Psychotria andersonii and also Psychotria andetrensis is not a commonly known plant however Psychotria andersonii is also known as  "the velvet leaf". The plant is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The health benefits of Psychotria andersonii include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria andersonii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, with the most common being andersonine and andersonine acetate. The most common herbal preparations made with Psychotria andersonii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Psychotria andetrensis is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family includes over 13,000 species, ranging from trees and shrubs to herbs and climbers. Psychotria andetrensis is a species of flowering plant, and within the Rubiaceae family, it is a member of the genus Psychotria. The genus Psychotria is further divided into several subgroups based on characteristics such as leaf shape and flower structure. The classification of Psychotria andetrensis into the family Rubiaceae and the genus Psychotria reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in these groups.
